---
title: "Knowledge Checks"
subtitle: "Test your understanding of each module"
---

```{=html}
<div class="canvas-reminder">
  <div class="canvas-reminder-body">
    <strong>These quizzes are for self-study practice only</strong>
    <p>The questions below mirror the graded quizzes on Canvas. To earn points toward your certificate, submit your responses through the <strong>Canvas course platform</strong>. Completing quizzes here does <em>not</em> count toward your grade.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Module 1: Citation Management Quiz

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-container" id="quiz-cm">

<div class="quiz-progress">
  <span>2 questions</span>
  <div class="quiz-progress-bar"><div class="quiz-progress-fill" style="width:0%" id="cm-progress"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">1. Which of the following is a key advantage of Zotero over a manual bibliography?</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="cm1" value="a"> It automatically formats references in any citation style and syncs across devices</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="cm1" value="b"> It only works with Google Docs</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="cm1" value="c"> It requires a paid subscription for all features</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="cm1" value="d"> It can only store 10 references at a time</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkCM1()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="cm1-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">2. True or False: Paperpile is best suited for researchers who primarily write in Google Docs.</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="cm2" value="true"> True</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="cm2" value="false"> False</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkCM2()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="cm2-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
var cmAnswered = 0;
function checkCM1() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="cm1"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('cm1-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'a') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Zotero automatically reformats your citations when you switch styles (e.g., APA to Chicago), saves to cloud storage, and syncs across all your devices — far more efficient than managing references by hand.';
    cmAnswered = Math.min(cmAnswered + 1, 2);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The correct answer is: <em>It automatically formats references in any citation style and syncs across devices.</em> Zotero supports thousands of citation styles and is free with 6 GB of cloud storage.';
  }
  document.getElementById('cm-progress').style.width = (cmAnswered / 2 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkCM2() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="cm2"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('cm2-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'true') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Paperpile was designed specifically for Google Docs integration. Its browser extension and Google Docs add-on make it the most seamless citation tool for that workflow.';
    cmAnswered = Math.min(cmAnswered + 1, 2);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> True — Paperpile excels in the Google Docs ecosystem. If you write primarily in Word or LibreOffice, Zotero may be a better fit.';
  }
  document.getElementById('cm-progress').style.width = (cmAnswered / 2 * 100) + '%';
}
</script>
```

---

## Module 2: Scholarly Communication Quiz

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-container" id="quiz-sc">

<div class="quiz-progress">
  <span>2 questions</span>
  <div class="quiz-progress-bar"><div class="quiz-progress-fill" style="width:0%" id="sc-progress"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">1. A researcher discovers that publications by someone with a similar name are appearing on their profile. What is the best next step?</div>
  <div style="font-size:0.88rem;color:#666;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">(Select all that apply)</div>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="sc1" value="a"> Ignore the issue</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="sc1" value="b"> Delete the entire profile</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="sc1" value="c"> Claim only verified works and use persistent identifiers</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="sc1" value="d"> Contact the journal editors</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkSC1()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="sc1-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">2. Your ORCID record should only include peer-reviewed articles.</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="sc2" value="true"> True</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="sc2" value="false"> False</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkSC2()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="sc2-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
var scAnswered = 0;
function checkSC1() {
  const checked = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('input[name="sc1"]:checked')).map(e => e.value);
  const fb = document.getElementById('sc1-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  const correct = checked.length === 1 && checked[0] === 'c';
  if (correct) {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Using persistent identifiers (like ORCID) and claiming only verified works is the best approach to disambiguate your research profile.';
    scAnswered = Math.min(scAnswered + 1, 2);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The best answer is: <em>Claim only verified works and use persistent identifiers</em>. ORCID is designed exactly for this problem — it gives you a unique identifier regardless of name ambiguity.';
  }
  document.getElementById('sc-progress').style.width = (scAnswered / 2 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkSC2() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="sc2"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('sc2-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'false') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Your ORCID record should include all types of scholarly outputs — peer-reviewed articles, preprints, datasets, presentations, and more.';
    scAnswered = Math.min(scAnswered + 1, 2);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> False — your ORCID record can and should include all types of scholarly outputs, not just peer-reviewed articles.';
  }
  document.getElementById('sc-progress').style.width = (scAnswered / 2 * 100) + '%';
}
</script>
```

---

## Module 3: Data Management Quiz

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-container" id="quiz-dm">

<div class="quiz-progress">
  <span>3 questions</span>
  <div class="quiz-progress-bar"><div class="quiz-progress-fill" style="width:0%" id="dm-progress"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">1. Fill in the blank: <strong>___</strong> is an online application that helps researchers write their data management plans.</div>
  <input type="text" id="dm1-input" placeholder="Type your answer" style="padding:0.4rem 0.7rem;border:2px solid #e0e7ef;border-radius:0.4rem;font-size:0.95rem;width:220px;">
  <br><br>
  <button onclick="checkDM1()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="dm1-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">2. Fill in the blanks: While a <strong>[A]</strong> provides a high-level overview of a project and instructions on how to use the files, a <strong>[B]</strong> defines the specific variables, units, and labels used within the dataset itself.</div>
  <strong>A:</strong> <input type="text" id="dm2a" placeholder="Type answer A" style="padding:0.4rem 0.7rem;border:2px solid #e0e7ef;border-radius:0.4rem;font-size:0.95rem;width:180px;"><br><br>
  <strong>B:</strong> <input type="text" id="dm2b" placeholder="Type answer B" style="padding:0.4rem 0.7rem;border:2px solid #e0e7ef;border-radius:0.4rem;font-size:0.95rem;width:180px;"><br><br>
  <button onclick="checkDM2()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="dm2-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">3. Define open and proprietary file formats. Which is generally preferred for long-term data sharing and archiving?</div>
  <textarea id="dm3-text" rows="4" style="width:100%;padding:0.6rem;border:2px solid #e0e7ef;border-radius:0.5rem;font-size:0.9rem;" placeholder="Write your answer here..."></textarea><br><br>
  <button onclick="checkDM3()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Submit (Self-Review)</button>
  <div id="dm3-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
var dmAnswered = 0;
function checkDM1() {
  const val = document.getElementById('dm1-input').value.trim().toLowerCase();
  const fb = document.getElementById('dm1-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  const correct = val.includes('dmp') || val.includes('dmptool');
  if (correct) {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> <strong>DMPTool</strong> (dmptool.org) is the standard online tool for writing data management plans, with funder-specific templates.';
    dmAnswered = Math.min(dmAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The answer is <strong>DMPTool</strong> — a free online application at dmptool.org with templates for NSF, NIH, and other funders.';
  }
  document.getElementById('dm-progress').style.width = (dmAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkDM2() {
  const a = document.getElementById('dm2a').value.trim().toLowerCase();
  const b = document.getElementById('dm2b').value.trim().toLowerCase();
  const fb = document.getElementById('dm2-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  const aOk = a.includes('readme') || a.includes('read me');
  const bOk = b.includes('codebook') || b.includes('code book');
  if (aOk && bOk) {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> A <strong>README</strong> gives a high-level project overview; a <strong>codebook</strong> defines variables and labels within the data.';
    dmAnswered = Math.min(dmAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> A = <strong>README</strong>; B = <strong>Codebook</strong>.';
  }
  document.getElementById('dm-progress').style.width = (dmAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkDM3() {
  const fb = document.getElementById('dm3-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  fb.innerHTML = `<div style="background:#e8f4fd;border:1px solid #b3d1e8;border-radius:0.4rem;padding:0.8rem;font-size:0.9rem;">
    <strong>Model Answer:</strong><br>
    <em>Open file formats</em> (e.g., .csv, .txt, .tiff) can be read by any software without a proprietary license. <em>Proprietary formats</em> (e.g., .xlsx, .docx, .psd) require specific commercial software to open.<br><br>
    <strong>Open formats are generally preferred</strong> for long-term data sharing and archiving because they ensure data remains accessible regardless of software availability in the future, and are required by many funding agencies and data repositories.
  </div>`;
  dmAnswered = Math.min(dmAnswered + 1, 3);
  document.getElementById('dm-progress').style.width = (dmAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
</script>
```

---

## Module 4: Literature Review Strategies Quiz

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-container" id="quiz-lr">

<div class="quiz-progress">
  <span>3 questions</span>
  <div class="quiz-progress-bar"><div class="quiz-progress-fill" style="width:0%" id="lr-progress"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">1. Which type of literature review follows a rigorous, pre-registered protocol to minimize bias and produce reproducible results, often used to answer clinical or policy questions?</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr1" value="a"> Narrative review</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr1" value="b"> Systematic review</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr1" value="c"> Annotated bibliography</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr1" value="d"> Scoping review</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkLR1()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="lr1-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">2. When constructing a database search strategy, which technique involves combining synonyms and related terms to broaden your results?</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr2" value="a"> Using AND between terms</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr2" value="b"> Using OR between terms</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr2" value="c"> Using NOT between terms</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr2" value="d"> Using quotation marks around a phrase</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkLR2()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="lr2-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">3. True or False: An annotated bibliography includes both a citation and a brief descriptive or evaluative paragraph for each source.</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr3" value="true"> True</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="lr3" value="false"> False</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkLR3()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="lr3-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
var lrAnswered = 0;
function checkLR1() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="lr1"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('lr1-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'b') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> A <strong>systematic review</strong> uses an explicit, reproducible methodology (often PRISMA guidelines), pre-registered search strategies, and strict inclusion/exclusion criteria to synthesize evidence on a focused question.';
    lrAnswered = Math.min(lrAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The answer is <strong>systematic review</strong>. A scoping review maps the evidence landscape without the same level of bias controls; a narrative review is more flexible and author-driven.';
  }
  document.getElementById('lr-progress').style.width = (lrAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkLR2() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="lr2"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('lr2-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'b') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> The Boolean operator <strong>OR</strong> broadens your search by retrieving records containing any of the combined terms (e.g., "anxiety OR stress OR worry"). Use AND to narrow results, and NOT to exclude terms.';
    lrAnswered = Math.min(lrAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> <strong>OR</strong> broadens a search by including synonyms. AND narrows results by requiring both terms; NOT excludes a term entirely.';
  }
  document.getElementById('lr-progress').style.width = (lrAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkLR3() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="lr3"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('lr3-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'true') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> An annotated bibliography pairs each citation with an annotation — a short paragraph that summarizes the source, evaluates its credibility, and explains its relevance to your research topic.';
    lrAnswered = Math.min(lrAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> True — each entry in an annotated bibliography contains the full citation plus a brief annotation describing and/or evaluating the source.';
  }
  document.getElementById('lr-progress').style.width = (lrAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
</script>
```

---

## Module 5: Publish & Share Your Research Quiz

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-container" id="quiz-pub">

<div class="quiz-progress">
  <span>2 questions</span>
  <div class="quiz-progress-bar"><div class="quiz-progress-fill" style="width:0%" id="pub-progress"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">1. Who typically owns the copyright to a graduate student's thesis or dissertation in the U.S.?</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="pub1" value="author"> The author (graduate student)</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="pub1" value="university"> The university</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="pub1" value="advisor"> The advisor</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="pub1" value="publisher"> The publisher</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkPub1()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="pub1-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">2. Which of the following materials may require permission to include in your thesis or dissertation? (Select all that apply)</div>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="pub2" value="photo"> Photographs taken by others</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="pub2" value="your-own"> Text you wrote yourself</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="pub2" value="journal-fig"> Figures from published journal articles</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="pub2" value="music"> Published music lyrics</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkPub2()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="pub2-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
var pubAnswered = 0;
function checkPub1() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="pub1"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('pub1-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'author') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> In the U.S., the graduate student author typically owns the copyright. Note: if you publish with a commercial publisher, your agreement may transfer some rights.';
    pubAnswered = Math.min(pubAnswered + 1, 2);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The correct answer is <strong>the author (graduate student)</strong>. Copyright is established at the moment of creation and belongs to you, unless you sign it away.';
  }
  document.getElementById('pub-progress').style.width = (pubAnswered / 2 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkPub2() {
  const checked = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('input[name="pub2"]:checked')).map(e => e.value);
  const fb = document.getElementById('pub2-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  const hasPhoto = checked.includes('photo');
  const hasJournal = checked.includes('journal-fig');
  const hasMusic = checked.includes('music');
  const hasOwn = checked.includes('your-own');
  const correct = hasPhoto && hasJournal && hasMusic && !hasOwn;
  if (correct) {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Photographs by others, journal figures, and published lyrics all require permission (or fair use analysis). Text you wrote yourself is already yours.';
    pubAnswered = Math.min(pubAnswered + 1, 2);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The items requiring permission are: <strong>photographs by others, figures from published articles, and published lyrics</strong>. Text you wrote yourself is already covered by your own copyright.';
  }
  document.getElementById('pub-progress').style.width = (pubAnswered / 2 * 100) + '%';
}
</script>
```

---

## Module 6: Graduate Academic Affairs Quiz

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-container" id="quiz-gaa">

<div class="quiz-progress">
  <span>3 questions</span>
  <div class="quiz-progress-bar"><div class="quiz-progress-fill" style="width:0%" id="gaa-progress"></div></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">1. Submitting your thesis or dissertation to ProQuest transfers your copyright to ProQuest.</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="gaa1" value="true"> True</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="gaa1" value="false"> False</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkGAA1()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="gaa1-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">2. Which of the following is a valid reason to request an embargo on your thesis or dissertation? (Select all that apply)</div>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="gaa2" value="a"> A publisher has requested exclusivity for publication</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="gaa2" value="b"> Your research contains sensitive data</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="gaa2" value="c"> You want to keep your research private forever</label><br>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="gaa2" value="d"> You are filing patents related to your work</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkGAA2()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="gaa2-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

<div class="quiz-question">
  <div class="question-text">3. When is the typical deadline for submitting your thesis or dissertation to the Graduate Division for degree conferral in a given quarter?</div>
  <label><input type="radio" name="gaa3" value="a"> The first day of the quarter</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="gaa3" value="b"> The last day of final exams</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="gaa3" value="c"> A specific filing deadline set by the Graduate Division, usually several weeks before the end of the quarter</label><br>
  <label><input type="radio" name="gaa3" value="d"> The day grades are due</label><br>
  <br>
  <button onclick="checkGAA3()" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Check Answer</button>
  <div id="gaa3-feedback" style="margin-top:0.7rem;display:none;"></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
var gaaAnswered = 0;
function checkGAA1() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="gaa1"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('gaa1-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'false') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Submitting to ProQuest does <strong>not</strong> transfer your copyright. The ProQuest agreement allows them to include your work in their database while you retain all copyright. You can still publish chapters with academic journals or presses.';
    gaaAnswered = Math.min(gaaAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> False — ProQuest\'s default agreement lets you <strong>retain copyright</strong>. They only receive a non-exclusive license to archive and make your work discoverable. Always read the agreement carefully before signing.';
  }
  document.getElementById('gaa-progress').style.width = (gaaAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkGAA2() {
  const checked = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('input[name="gaa2"]:checked')).map(e => e.value);
  const fb = document.getElementById('gaa2-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  const hasA = checked.includes('a');
  const hasB = checked.includes('b');
  const hasC = checked.includes('c');
  const hasD = checked.includes('d');
  const correct = hasA && hasB && hasD && !hasC;
  if (correct) {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> Valid embargo reasons include publisher exclusivity, sensitive data, and pending patents. Embargoes are always <em>temporary</em> — wanting permanent privacy is not a valid basis for an embargo.';
    gaaAnswered = Math.min(gaaAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> Valid reasons are: <strong>publisher exclusivity, sensitive data, and filing patents</strong>. Embargoes are temporary delays (usually 1–2 years), not permanent restrictions.';
  }
  document.getElementById('gaa-progress').style.width = (gaaAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
function checkGAA3() {
  const val = document.querySelector('input[name="gaa3"]:checked');
  const fb = document.getElementById('gaa3-feedback');
  fb.style.display = 'block';
  if (!val) { fb.innerHTML = 'Please select an answer.'; return; }
  if (val.value === 'c') {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#2e7d32;font-weight:600;">✅ Correct!</span> The Graduate Division sets specific filing deadlines each quarter — typically several weeks before the end of the quarter. Missing this deadline means your degree conferral is pushed to the next quarter. Always check <a href="https://graduate.ucr.edu/filing-resources" target="_blank">graduate.ucr.edu/filing-resources</a> for current dates.';
    gaaAnswered = Math.min(gaaAnswered + 1, 3);
  } else {
    fb.innerHTML = '<span style="color:#c62828;font-weight:600;">Not quite.</span> The correct answer is <strong>a specific filing deadline set by the Graduate Division</strong>, usually several weeks before quarter end. These dates are posted on the Graduate Division website each term.';
  }
  document.getElementById('gaa-progress').style.width = (gaaAnswered / 3 * 100) + '%';
}
</script>
```

---

::: {.callout-tip}
### Note on Self-Paced Quizzes
These interactive questions are for **self-study**. For official certification, complete all module assignments on Canvas and submit the [Capstone Research Plan](modules/07-capstone.qmd) to your instructor.
:::
